title: <name> and Robo Under the Mango Tree

pages.text: One sunny evening in her small Indian town, <name> sat under the big mango tree, looking sadly at her broken glowing kite. “Oh no,” she sighed, “it won’t fly at the school fair tomorrow!”
Just then, she heard a whirr-whirr-click! From behind the tree rolled a shiny little robot with blinking eyes. “Hello, <name>! I’m Robo! Need a hand?” he beeped cheerfully.
<name> smiled. “Yes, please, Robo! My magic kite won’t glow or fly anymore.” Robo opened a tiny toolbox in his tummy. “No problem! Let’s fix it together!”
He buzzed and tinkered — zrrr-tik-tik-tik! — while <name> held the kite steady. “Maybe it needs a spark of starlight,” she said. Robo nodded. “Or a sprinkle of imagination!”

He touched the kite with his glowing finger, and suddenly the stars above twinkled brighter. The kite shimmered in rainbow colors and lifted into the night sky — whoooosh! It sparkled above the rooftops and the school playground, leaving a trail of glittering light.
“Wow!” cried <name>. “It’s even better than before!” Robo spun happily. “Mission complete!” Amma called from the balcony, “<name>! Time for dinner!” <name> waved to her new friend. “Thank you, Robo!”

As Robo rolled away under the mango tree, he blinked one last light — beep-beep! — and whispered, “See you at the next invention!” The glowing kite danced high in the sky as <name> smiled, knowing new adventures awaited tomorrow.
